free_text: Full paper of contribution to Symposium on Neutron
Cross Sections from 10-50 MeV, held at Upton,
Deuteron-deuterium reaction in a 10 cm long
gaseous target containing deuterium at 1 atm.
Detector efficiency was calibrated using a 252-Cf
source assuming a value of NU=3.732 and Maxwellian
Neutron detection efficiency as function of energy
was estimated by Monte Carlo calculations .
The efficiency energy dependence was smoothed as
Au, and Bi, (and Lu and Ta).
Separated isotopes for the even isotopes of Se,Nd,
Sm,Eu-151, and the main isotopes of Gd,W,Tl and
Lu-175 and Ta-181 are only the major components of
Calculation by S.Pearlstein, Nuclear Data B,
Section A, 3(1967) used for correcting Se-74 and
Se-77 contributions and to derive (n,2n) cross-
About 15 gram. disks of 25 mm diam. Metallic
samples except for separated isotopes of Se, Sm,
Nd and Gd, which were oxides.
Efficiency correction for difference of Cf252 fission
For events not induced in sample.
For the effect of (n,2n) reaction in the axial tube
of scintillator tank - estimated to be negligible.
For reactions induced by degraded neutrons ( -3%)
and loss of secondary neutrons in sample ( +0.5%) .
The direct detection technique of emitted neutrons
used in this experiment is not limited to nuclei
residual isotopes as it is the case for activation
data published in J.Frehaut et al., INDC(USA)-84, p.399
with Frehaut). Data compiled in this EXFOR Entry
(Subents 002-062) are original ones not corrected by
data updated with the Upton Conference Paper.
SUBWORK 005 deleted, as Ni-data were with-
M.M. Page and volume numbers were added in
reference C,75KIEV. Author name G.Mosinski was added
from C,75KIEV. Page was added in C,75GAUSSIG.
SD: CRITIQUE was added. Ref. on private
C,75WASH; page was corrected in C,75GAUSSIG. Data
Upper -> lower case corrections in Subents 023-062.
Initial data were superseded by revised data.
1983-11-22 Data ( Ni-0(n,2n) CS) were never presented
at the Washington Conference (line in this Subent 005)
SD: CEA-R-6447 moved on te first place.
Errors given are standard errors derived by
quadratic addition of the statistical errors of the
experimental data and the errors of the correction.
